The Federal Compliance Digest provides official guidance on commercial internet monitoring requirements under Federal Mandate 2000-CR7. All operators are required to maintain awareness of current regulations.

items.a: Federal Mandate 2000-CR7 establishes monitoring and compliance requirements for all commercial internet operators. Full text available at focs.gov.net.
All registered operators and businesses using CorpOS-certified systems must maintain compliance. Non-compliance may result in fines, license review, or federal audit.

items.q: How do I check my compliance status?
items.a: Log into the CorpOS Operator Portal at corpos.gov.net with your operator credentials. Your compliance score is displayed on the dashboard.
Audits may be triggered by: elevated notoriety scores, insufficient operating funds, failure to file quarterly reports, or random selection.

items.q: Where can I get help with compliance?
items.a: Contact the Compliance Monitor hotline at 1-800-COMPLY or submit a request through the CorpOS Operator Portal.

title: Y2K Prep
siteTagline: Were You Prepared? — A Y2K Bug Archive

y2k.marquee: The millennium bug was REAL! This site archives all Y2K preparedness resources.

sections.body: Y2KPrep.net was originally created in 1998 to help people prepare for the Year 2000 computer bug. Now that we survived, this site serves as an archive of one of the biggest technology scares in history.

The Year 2000 Problem was a computer flaw caused by storing years as two digits. When clocks rolled from 99 to 00, many feared systems would fail catastrophically. Governments and corporations spent an estimated $300 billion on remediation.
